insight: For South Africa in the FIFA World Cup 2026, hydration breaks have had a mixed impact on their gameplay momentum. On average, they tend to lose a bit of momentum after a hydration break, with a mean change of -0.050 per minute, indicating a slight decline in their performance. Notably, when they weren't pressing before a break, they maintained this average decline, suggesting that the breaks themselves might be disrupting their rhythm rather than providing a significant boost. Overall, the numbers suggest that South Africa struggles to regain their footing after hydration breaks, which could be an area for the team to focus on in order to improve their overall performance in the tournament.
